home.title: Home
home.confirmUninstall:
Are you sure you want to uninstall "{appName}"?

This action will remove:
- Docker containers
- Application data
- Configuration files

This action is irreversible.
home.timeoutError: Timeout exceeded - operation takes more than 2 minutes
home.weatherCityDescription: You can use your current location (automatic) or set a city.

icon.resetAccessMessage: The owner account will be reset (your data is kept). You will need to set up a new account on next open.
icon.confirmUninstallMessage: All data will be permanently deleted.

defaultCredentials.title: Default account
defaultCredentials.warning: Please change the username and password for security.

appSettings.title: Settings
appSettings.notAvailable: Public exposure is not available for this app.
appSettings.managedNatively: This app's public address is managed automatically by Ryvie.
appSettings.notExposed: This app has no public address: it is only accessible to Ryvie users.
appSettings.exposedHint: This app is reachable from the Internet at this address.
appSettings.createWarning: The application will be reachable from the Internet by anyone. Make sure every account has a strong password. The application may restart automatically during the operation.
appSettings.creatingHint: Creating the address can take up to 2 minutes.
appSettings.createdPending: Public address created. It will be active in a few moments.
appSettings.createError: Unable to create the public address. Please try again later.
appSettings.deleteWarning: The application will no longer be reachable from the Internet. It may restart automatically during the operation.
appSettings.deletingHint: Removing the address can take up to 2 minutes.
appSettings.deleteError: Unable to remove the public address. Please try again later.
appSettings.envUpdated: The application is restarting to apply the change.
appSettings.infoText: A public address (*.ryvie.fr) makes this app reachable from the Internet, outside your Ryvie network. Generation goes through our cloud service and can take up to 2 minutes; the app may restart automatically to apply the change. You can close this window: creation keeps running in the background and the app icon shows a spinner until the address is active. Make sure every account is protected by a strong password before exposing the app.

appAccounts.title: Accounts
appAccounts.unsupported: Password management is not available for this app.
appAccounts.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
appAccounts.resetRestartWarn: Resetting the password restarts the app (a few seconds of downtime).

updateModal.takingLonger: Server is taking longer than expected...
updateModal.restartTakingLong: Restart is taking longer than expected. Please refresh the page manually in a moment.
updateModal.autoReload: The page will automatically reload as soon as the system is ready.

appStore.title: Discover the App Store
appStore.subtitle: Install your favorite applications
appStore.description: The App Store lets you easily install all your favorite applications.

step3.title: Launch and enjoy
step3.description: Your applications appear on your home screen

desktop.title: Ryvie Desktop
desktop.description: Access your Ryvie from anywhere in the world. Ryvie Desktop establishes an ultra-secure connection between your device and your personal server, wherever you are.

connect.title: Ryvie Connect
connect.description: Available in the App Store, Ryvie Connect lets you connect to your Ryvie from your smartphone.

rpictures.title: rPictures
rpictures.description: Automatically backup your photos and videos to your Ryvie. rPictures is also available in the App Store.

manageApps.title: Manage Your Applications
manageApps.subtitle: Easily control your installed apps
manageApps.description: A simple right-click on an application gives you access to all management options.

step1.title: Start / Stop
step1.description: Control the status of your applications with one click

step2.title: Restart
step2.description: Restart an application in case of problems

step3.title: Uninstall
step3.description: Remove applications you no longer need

personalize.title: Personalize Your Space
personalize.subtitle: Make Ryvie your home
personalize.description: Customize your experience to make it your own.

step2.title: Organization
step2.description: Move and organize your applications as you wish

step3.title: Widgets
step3.description: Add weather, storage, and more widgets

step4.title: Theme
step4.description: Switch between light and dark mode

ready.title: You're Ready!
ready.subtitle: Start your adventure with Ryvie
ready.description: You now have all the keys to fully enjoy Ryvie!
ready.tipsTitle: A few tips to get started:
ready.tip1: Explore the App Store to install your first applications
ready.tip3: Organize your applications by moving them on the screen
ready.tip4: Check the documentation if you have questions
ready.readyMessage: Ready to discover your new personal space?

settings.title: Cloud Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure your server
settings.backgroundDescription: Customize your home page background. You can add multiple backgrounds.
settings.downloadFolderDescription: Location where downloaded files will be saved

ai.title: AI Provider
ai.description: Connect an LLM provider once, then choose which apps use it. Ryvie runs a private gateway (LiteLLM). Your provider key is never exposed to the apps.
ai.modelHint: Type any model name supported by the provider. The list is only suggestions.
ai.savedPending: Saved. The AI gateway is still starting…
ai.saveWarn: Saving restarts the gateway: AI for connected apps is interrupted for a few seconds.
ai.saveError: Unable to save the AI configuration.
ai.loadError: Unable to load the AI settings.
ai.aliasNote: Once saved, your apps see this model as a standard OpenAI model (e.g. “gpt-4o”, “ryvie-ai”), whatever provider or actual model you picked. The gateway handles the translation.
ai.cliNotInstalled: Claude CLI is not installed on this machine
ai.cliLoginIntro: Open the authorization page, sign in to your Claude account, then paste the code you get back here.
ai.configureFirst: Configure a provider above to connect apps.
ai.appError: Unable to update the app connection.
ai.confirmWarn: ⚠️ The app will restart; any running tasks/services will be interrupted.

settings.sessionDescription: Reconnection required after 60 minutes of inactivity
settings.accessModeDescription: Defines how the application connects to the Ryvie server
settings.publicAddressesDescription: Your applications are accessible via these public addresses
settings.vpnPeersDescription: Devices connected to the Ryvie VPN network (peers)
settings.vpnPeersEmpty: No device detected on the VPN network
settings.connectDeviceTitle: Connect a device to your Ryvie
settings.connectDeviceDescription: Run the command below on the device you want to connect: it installs NetBird and links it to your Ryvie automatically.
settings.connectDeviceHint: Once connected, the device will appear under "Ryvie VPN network devices".
settings.confirmDeleteBackgroundMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this background?
settings.sessionDurationChanged: Session duration changed: {minutes} minute{minutes, plural, =1 {} other{s}}
settings.connectivityFailed: ❌ Cannot access server in {mode} mode. Check your connection.
settings.twoFactorNotAvailable: Two-factor authentication is not yet available
settings.confirmUpdateRyvieMessage: Are you sure you want to update Ryvie? The server will automatically restart after the update.
settings.confirmUpdateAppMessage: Are you sure you want to update {appName}? The application will automatically restart after the update.
settings.setupKeyDescription: Configuration key to connect new devices to your Ryvie from anywhere in the world
settings.thisMayTakeFewSeconds: This may take a few seconds
settings.restartSystemDescription: This action will perform a complete system reboot. All services will be interrupted for a few minutes.

storageSettings.raidAssistantDesc: View your disks, their health status, and configure your RAID
storageSettings.selectDiskToAdd: Select a disk to add to RAID
storageSettings.diskWillBeErased: The disk will be erased, partitioned (GPT), and added as a member of the RAID
storageSettings.noRaidDetected: No mdadm RAID detected on /data. You can create one in the Create RAID tab.
storageSettings.resyncBlocksMigration: Please wait for synchronization to complete before starting a new migration.
storageSettings.confirmStopResync: Are you sure you want to stop the ongoing synchronization?
storageSettings.resyncPausedDesc: The RAID was restarted in read-only mode. Click to resume synchronization.
storageSettings.setupContinueDesc: Once the RAID is configured, you can proceed to create your user account.
storageSettings.diskHealthDesc: SMART data, temperature, power-on hours and reallocated sectors
storageSettings.chooseRaidLevelDesc: Select the RAID type based on your performance and redundancy needs
storageSettings.selectDisksDesc: Click on available disks to include them in the new RAID array
storageSettings.raid10EvenRequired: RAID 10 requires an even number of disks
storageSettings.noLogsYet: No logs yet. Configure and execute a RAID operation to see logs here.
storageSettings.optimizationWarning: This operation involves temporarily unmounting /data and modifying partitions. Make sure you have backups.
storageSettings.diskWillBeErasedWarning: Disk {disk} will be completely erased. All data will be lost.
storageSettings.createRaidWarning: All selected disks will be erased and partitioned. All data will be lost. Make sure you have backups.
storageSettings.memberLimitsCapacity: Member {device} ({size}) limits RAID capacity. Consider removing it to use only larger disks.
storageSettings.reshapeDesc: Your array is currently {{currentLevel}} with {{disks}} disks. You can convert it to another RAID level without data loss.
storageSettings.reshapeWarning: RAID conversion may take several hours depending on disk size. The RAID remains accessible during the operation, but performance will be reduced. Make sure you have backups before proceeding.
storageSettings.raidMembersDesc: Current disks in the array. You can remove a member to degrade the array (e.g., before growing or replacing a disk).
storageSettings.removeDiskWarning: This will remove {{partition}} from the RAID array. The array will become degraded and redundancy will be reduced. Make sure you have at least 2 active members before removing one.
storageSettings.growArrayDesc: Expand RAID and filesystem to use full member capacity
storageSettings.growArrayExplanation: The RAID members are larger than the current array size. This operation will grow the RAID to use the full partition size, then resize the btrfs filesystem. No data loss, no downtime — works live.
storageSettings.autoMigrateDesc: Select the target RAID level and disks. Migration will run automatically step by step.
storageSettings.selectDisksToMigrateDesc: Select available disks for the migration
storageSettings.migrationWarning: This will modify the existing RAID array. Selected disks will be erased and added to the RAID. Data on /data is preserved throughout the operation. This can take several hours.
storageSettings.migrationAlreadyRunning: A migration is already in progress
storageSettings.notEnoughDisksForLevel: RAID{{level}} requires at least {{min}} disks total

raidLevelDesc.raid0: Maximum performance with no redundancy. If one disk fails, all data is lost.
raidLevelDesc.raid1: Full mirror. Data is copied to each disk. Excellent safety.
raidLevelDesc.raid5: Good performance/safety compromise. Data is distributed with parity.
raidLevelDesc.raid6: Like RAID 5 but tolerates 2 simultaneous disk failures.
raidLevelDesc.raid10: Mirror + stripe combination. High performance and good redundancy.

connexion.errorLoadingUsers: Error loading users. Please check your server connection.

connexion.windowOpened: Window opened for {userName} in {accessMode} mode with role {userRole}
